title: crocus atticus subsp. sublimis
intro: Crocus atticus subsp. sublimis, also known as the Greek crocus, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Iridaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, with its health benefits attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The herbal preparations of Crocus atticus subsp. sublimis include extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical and internal use.
